require 'set'

def solve(points, target)
    # 1. Check K=0
    target_str = target.join(',')
    all_points_set = Set.new
    
    points.each do |p|
        p_str = p.join(',')
        all_points_set.add(p_str)
        if p.join(',') == target_str
            return 0
        end
    end

    # Edge case: only one point initially, no pairs can be formed.
    if points.length < 2
        return -1
    end

    k = 0
    
    loop do
        k += 1
        
        # Convert all known point strings back to arrays for processing (S_{k-1})
        current_points = all_points_set.map do |s|
            s.split(',').map(&:to_i)
        end
        
        next_generation_keys = Set.new
        n = current_points.length
        
        # Generate all pairs (p1, p2) where p1 and p2 are distinct points from S_{k-1}
        current_points.each_with_index do |p1, i|
            (i + 1).upto(n - 1) do |j|
                p2 = current_points[j]
                
                # Calculate average point c = [floor((x1 + x2)/2), ...]
                # Use // for explicit floor division on non-negative integers.
                c = [
                    (p1[0] + p2[0]) // 2,
                    (p1[1] + p2[1]) // 2,
                    (p1[2] + p2[2]) // 2
                ]
                
                c_key = c.join(',')
                
                # Check if this generated point is the target
                if c_key == target_str
                    return k
                end
                
                # Only collect it if it's genuinely new to the entire collection
                if !all_points_set.include?(c_key)
                    next_generation_keys.add(c_key)
                end
            end
        end
        
        # If no new points were generated, we stop.
        if next_generation_keys.empty?
            return -1
        end
        
        # Add all newly generated, unique points to the master set S_k
        next_generation_keys.each do |key|
            all_points_set.add(key)
        end
    end
end

    # Helper function to serialize a point [x, y, z] into a unique string key
    serialize_point = ->(p) { p.join(',') }
    
    target_key = serialize_point(target)

    all_points_set = {}

        if p_key == target_key
            return 0
        end
    end

    # If only one initial point exists, no new points can ever be generated.
    if points.length < 2
        return -1
    end

    k = 0
    new_points_generated = true

    # Iteratively generate subsequent generations (k >= 1)
    while new_points_generated
        k += 1
        new_points_generated = false
        
        # Convert the set keys to an array of actual point arrays for pairing
        current_points_array = all_points_set.keys.map { |s| s.split(',').map(&:to_i) }
        
        # Temporary storage for genuinely new points discovered in this generation
        next_generation_new_keys = {}

        N = current_points_array.length

        (0...N).each do |i|

                p1 = current_points_array[i]
                p2 = current_points_array[j]

                # Calculate the average point c = [floor((x1 + x2)/2), ...]

                c = [

                    (p1[1] + p2[1]) / 2,
                    (p1[2] + p2[2]) / 2
                ]
                
                c_key = serialize_point(c)

                # Check if this generated point is the target
                if c_key == target_key
                    return k
                end

                # Only consider adding it to the next batch if it is genuinely new to the entire collection
                unless all_points_set.key?(c_key)
                    next_generation_new_keys[c_key] = true
                end
            end
        end

        # Merge genuinely new points into the master set
        if !next_generation_new_keys.empty?
            new_points_generated = true
            next_generation_new_keys.each do |new_key|
                all_points_set[new_key] = true
            end
        end
    end

    # If the loop finishes without finding the target
    return -1
